summary: Do not ship as-is: the helper locks in the wrong Gary bias semantics, is not wired into live post-resolve routing, and its type contract admits unsupported role values.

findings.title: Gary's "fast lean" is implemented as a no-op and the tests codify the wrong baseline
findings.recommendation: Fix the floor/prefer encoding or the helper semantics so Gary can actually bias a baseline `standard` route toward `fast`, then update the vitest baseline to expect that mode->role mapping instead of asserting Gary stays `standard`.

findings.title: The bias helper is not applied to live routing after `resolveRoute`; this slice only injects prompt guidance
findings.recommendation: Wire the post-resolve role bias into the gateway routing path, re-map the biased role through the active profile's `roleMap`, and add route-level tests that verify mode->resolved target changes without mutating router config.

findings.title: `ModeBias` accepts the full `Role` vocabulary even though the implementation only supports compute-tier roles
findings.recommendation: Narrow `floor`/`prefer` to a compute-role subtype (`fast|standard|expert`) or add validation that rejects `image|video|review`; otherwise invalid bias configs type-check and silently no-op.
